name: Stranger Things
description: Hawkins, the Upside Down, and the kids of Mike's party

questions.q: In which decade is 'Stranger Things' set?

source.notes: Season 1 begins in November 1983. Filled with 80s pop culture references.

questions.q: In which fictional town is the show set?

source.notes: Hawkins, Indiana. Fictional setting, actually filmed in Atlanta, Georgia.

questions.q: In which U.S. state is Hawkins located?

source.notes: In 'real' world Hawkins is Jackson, Georgia. Stranger Things helped boost Georgia's film industry.

questions.q: Who are the creators of 'Stranger Things'?

source.original_a: The Duffer Brothers (Matt and Ross Duffer)
source.notes: Matt and Ross Duffer, identical twins born 1984.

questions.q: In what year did 'Stranger Things' first premiere on Netflix?

source.notes: July 15, 2016. Initial title was 'Montauk.'

questions.q: What telekinetic ability does Eleven have?

source.notes: She can move objects with her mind. Also has psychic 'Void' abilities.

questions.q: What is the name of the faceless monster from the Upside Down in Season 1?

source.notes: Named after a D&D monster. Has a flower-like mouth.

questions.q: Who plays Eleven on the show?

source.notes: She was 11 when cast (and the show was filmed). Born in Spain.

questions.q: Which actress plays Will Byers's mother, Joyce?

source.notes: Sparked a career resurgence for Ryder, dubbed 'The Winona-ssance.'

questions.q: Who plays the gruff-but-loving Police Chief Jim Hopper?

source.notes: Harbour's breakthrough role. Later played Red Guardian in Marvel films.

questions.q: Which character is the leader and 'D&D game master' of the kids' group?

source.notes: Mike Wheeler, played by Finn Wolfhard. Organizes Eleven's initial hiding.

questions.q: What Kate Bush song famously resurged in popularity thanks to Season 4?

source.notes: 1985 original reached #1 in UK in 2022 thanks to the show. 'A Deal with God.'

questions.q: What character in Season 4 becomes the main antagonist with a name borrowed from D&D?

source.notes: Played by Jamie Campbell Bower. Originally Henry Creel.

questions.q: What is the name of the ice cream parlor where Steve and Robin work in Season 3?

source.notes: Located in the Starcourt Mall. Nautical sailor uniforms.

questions.q: What large shopping center dominates Hawkins in Season 3?

source.notes: Based on Gwinnett Place Mall in Georgia. Hides Soviet secret lab.

questions.q: What role-playing game do the kids play in Mike's basement?

source.notes: D&D. Many monsters named after D&D creatures (Demogorgon, Vecna, Mind Flayer).

questions.q: How does Joyce Byers communicate with Will when he's trapped in the Upside Down?

source.notes: She paints letters on the wall and uses the lights to form words.

questions.q: What arcade game does Dustin master and post a high score on in Season 2?

source.notes: His score is beaten by Max Mayfield, who uses the handle 'MADMAX.'

questions.q: Who are the Russian soldiers/scientists opening a gate in Season 3?

source.notes: Hide their lab beneath Starcourt Mall. Grigori hunts Hopper.

questions.q: What is the name of Nancy Wheeler's best friend who dies in Season 1?

source.notes: Played by Shannon Purser. '#JusticeForBarb' became a major social media movement.
